cards.question: What does @SpringBootApplication include under the hood?
What is auto-configuration and how does it work?
cards.answer: Spring Boot auto-configuration creates beans based on classpath dependencies, configuration properties, and conditional annotations.

cards.question: How can you override an auto-configured bean?
cards.answer: Define your own bean of the same type or name in configuration; Spring usually backs off when a user-defined bean exists.

cards.question: What are Actuator endpoints used for?
cards.answer: Actuator exposes production-ready endpoints for health, metrics, environment, thread dumps, and application diagnostics.

cards.question: Difference between application.properties and application.yml?
cards.answer: Both define configuration, but YAML is more hierarchical and concise for nested settings, while properties are key-value flat entries.

cards.question: How do profiles work in Spring Boot?
cards.answer: Profiles allow environment-specific configuration; activate them using spring.profiles.active or profile-specific config files.
